assumptions: The intragroup dividend gives rise to a foreign-currency-denominated item or transaction exposure in the consolidated financial statements.
The question is about hedge accounting under IFRS 9 at consolidated level.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities, or components of them, exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including foreign currency risk in eligible cases.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
